info.name: KikooAI Backend
info.description:
Complete API collection for the KikooAI English learning + interview prep platform backend.

**Setup:**
1. Set `baseUrl` variable to `http://localhost:3000`
2. Run **Signup** or **Login** — tokens are auto-saved.
3. All authenticated routes use `{{accessToken}}` automatically.

**Audio Flow:**
`POST /audio/upload-init` → upload directly to Cloudinary → `POST /audio/complete` → poll `GET /jobs/:id/status` → `GET /audio/:id/transcript`

request.description: Checks server status and database connectivity. No auth required.

Register a new user. Role must be one of: `student`, `job_seeker`, `professional`.

Auto-saves `accessToken` and `refreshToken` to collection variables.

response.name: 409 Duplicate user
400 Validation Error

request.description: Authenticate an existing user. Auto-saves tokens to collection variables.
Rotate the refresh token. Old token is invalidated, new pair is issued.
Invalidates all refresh tokens for the authenticated user.
Returns the authenticated user joined with their full profile (XP, streak, badges, subscription, etc.).
Update profile fields. All fields optional.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Fetch a random exercise seed. `answer_key` and `acceptable_variants` are **stripped** before returning.

**Valid types:** `fillup`, `jumbled_word`, `jumbled_sentence`, `vocab`, `synonyms`, `antonyms`, `pronunciation_spelling`, `grammar_transform`, `typing_from_audio`

response.name: 200 OK
404 No exercises found
200 OK

request.description:
Submit an answer for any exercise type.

- `user_answer`: string for text types, option ID (`"a"`,`"b"`,`"c"`,`"d"`) for vocab/synonyms/antonyms
- Consumes 1 energy, awards XP, updates streak if threshold met.

response.name: 200 Correct
200 Wrong
402 Energy Depleted

request.description:
Returns a speaking prompt matched to the user's current XP level.

| XP | CEFR | Difficulty |
|---|---|---|
| 0–300 | A1/A2 | easy |
| 301–1500 | B1/B2 | medium |
| 1501+ | C1 | hard |

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Evaluate a speaking exercise submission.

- `audio_id`: UUID from a completed audio transcription
- `prompt_id`: UUID of the speaking prompt seed

Reuses existing `feedback_json` if already analysed. Composite score = average of pronunciation + vocabulary + grammar + fluency. Awards XP and updates streak.

response.name: 200 OK — Evaluated
202 Transcript Still Processing

request.description:
**Step 1 of the audio flow.**

Generates a signed Cloudinary upload URL. The client should use the returned params to upload the audio file **directly to Cloudinary** — it never passes through the backend server.

**context_type** values: `speaking` | `interview` | `speed_reading`

**format** values: `webm` | `mp4` | `mp3` | `wav` | `ogg` | `m4a`

The signature is valid for 15 minutes (`expires_in_seconds: 900`).

**Step 2 — done by the client, NOT through this backend.**

Upload the audio file directly to Cloudinary using the params from Step 1.

Cloudinary will return a JSON response containing `public_id` and `secure_url` — save these for Step 3.

Example Cloudinary response:
```json
{
  "public_id": "kikoo/audio/user-id/abc123",
  "secure_url": "https://res.cloudinary.com/dv0xgum7s/video/upload/v1234/kikoo/audio/abc123.webm",
  "duration": 45.2,
  "format": "webm"
}
```

**Step 3 of the audio flow.**

Tell the backend about the completed Cloudinary upload. The backend will:
1. Verify the asset exists on Cloudinary
2. Update the `audio_files` record
3. Enqueue a transcription job (Gemini)

Auto-saves `audioId` and `jobId` to collection variables.

response.name: 404 Audio record not found / wrong owner

request.description:
**Step 4 — poll every 2–3 seconds until `status` is `done` or `failed`.**

Progress stages:
- `10%` — Downloading audio from Cloudinary
- `30%` — Audio downloaded
- `60%` — Transcription complete
- `80%` — Feedback analysis complete
- `100%` — Done, transcript saved

Jobs are user-scoped — returns 404 for jobs that belong to a different user.

**Step 5 — fetch after job status is `done`.**

Returns the full transcript and structured AI feedback.

If transcription is not yet complete, returns `202` with the current status.

response.name: 200 Done — Full transcript

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's 20 most recent audio files, ordered by creation date descending.

Permanently deletes the audio file from Cloudinary and all associated records from the database (audio_files + transcripts cascade).

Used for GDPR deletion requests.

item.name: Resumes
item.description:
Resume upload (file or JSON), AI analysis, roast, and management.

**File upload flow:**
`POST /resumes/upload-init` → upload directly to Cloudinary raw endpoint → `POST /resumes/upload-complete` → use `resume_id` in /analyze or /roast → poll `/resumes/reports/:report_id`

**JSON flow:**
`POST /resumes/save-json` → use `resume_id` directly in /analyze or /roast

request.description:
**Step 1 of the resume file upload flow.**

Generates a signed Cloudinary upload URL for raw files (PDF/DOCX/TXT). No body required.

Creates a placeholder `resumes` row and returns its `resume_id` so you can reference it in /upload-complete.

The upload URL targets Cloudinary's `/raw/upload` endpoint.

**Step 2 — Upload directly to Cloudinary.**

This request goes to Cloudinary, NOT the backend. Use the params returned from upload-init.

Save `public_id` and `secure_url` from the response for the next step.

**Step 3 — Confirm the Cloudinary upload.**

Verifies the asset exists on Cloudinary and updates the resume record.

`format`: `pdf` | `docx` | `txt`
`title` is optional (max 100 chars).

response.name: 400 File not on Cloudinary

request.description:
Save structured resume data as JSON — no file upload needed.

Auto-saves `resumeId` to collection variables.

Trigger an ATS-style AI analysis of the resume against a job description.

`jd_text` must be at least 50 characters.
`cover_letter` is optional.

Auto-saves `reportId` and `jobId`.

Poll `/resumes/reports/:report_id` for results.

response.name: 400 JD too short

request.description:
Same as analyze but returns witty, sharp constructive roast comments in `roast_lines`.

Auto-saves `reportId` and `jobId`.

Poll for analysis results.

- **202** — still processing
- **200** — done, full report available

response.name: 200 Done — analyze
200 Done — roast

request.description: Returns up to 20 most recent resumes for the authenticated user (newest first).

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Permanently deletes the resume and all associated reports. If a file resume, also removes from Cloudinary.

item.name: Interview
item.description:
Mock interview rooms with AI-scored per-question feedback.

**Full flow:**
`POST /interview/rooms/create` → record audio via audio pipeline → `POST /rooms/:id/record/start` → `POST /rooms/:id/record/stop` with audio_id → poll `/jobs/:job_id/status` → `GET /rooms/:id/result`

**Quick evaluate (no room):**
`POST /interview/questions/evaluate` with question_text + answer_text

request.description:
Create a new interview room. All fields optional.

- `difficulty`: `easy` | `medium` | `hard`
- `questions`: max 20 items

Auto-saves `roomId` to collection variables.

response.name: 201 Created

request.description:
Mark the interview room as recording. No body required.

Call this when the user clicks Start — use this timestamp for the UI timer.

response.name: 400 Already active

request.description:
Stop recording and trigger AI processing.

Requires `audio_id` from the audio pipeline (`POST /audio/complete`).

Auto-saves `jobId`. Poll `/jobs/:job_id/status` then fetch result.

Fetch the interview result. Returns `result: null` if not done yet.

Poll `/jobs/:job_id/status` first and only call this when status = done.

response.name: 200 Done

request.description: Returns up to 20 most recent interview rooms for the authenticated user.

Evaluate any question + answer immediately — no room needed.

At least one of `answer_text` or `audio_id` required.

Great for browser extensions or quick practice.

response.name: 200 OK
400 No answer provided

item.name: Jobs
item.description:
Background job management. All AI tasks (transcription, resume analysis, interview scoring) run as background jobs.

Use `GET /jobs/:job_id/status` to poll a specific job and `GET /jobs` to list all recent jobs.

request.description:
Returns the 20 most recent jobs for the authenticated user (newest first).

Includes all job types: `transcription`, `resume_analyze`, `interview_score`.
